stem: Find the area swept by the polar curve \( r=[[a]]\theta \) for \( \theta\in[0,[[b]]] \): \( \frac{1}{2}\int_0^{[[b]]} r^{2}\,d\theta \).
A circular sector is bounded by the polar curve \( r=[[a]] \) and the rays \( \theta=0 \) and \( \theta=\dfrac{[[m]]\pi}{[[n]]} \). Find the area of the sector.
solution: For constant \( r=[[a]] \), the sector area is \( \frac{1}{2}r^{2}\theta=\frac{1}{2}([[a]])^{2}\cdot\frac{[[m]]\pi}{[[n]]}=[[answer]] \); no integration is needed since \( r \) does not depend on \( \theta \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the \( \tfrac12 \) factor from the sector-area formula.
This uses \( r \) instead of \( r^{2}\) in the formula.
This forgets to include \( \pi \) when converting the angle.
This uses the full-circle area formula instead of the sector's central angle.
This is off by an additive constant from the correct sector area.
This uses \( \tfrac14 r^2\theta \) instead of \( \tfrac12 r^2\theta \).

stem: Find the total area enclosed by the cardioid \( r=[[a]](1+\cos\theta) \) as \( \theta \) ranges over \( [0,2\pi] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the area of a circle of radius \( a \) instead of expanding \( (1+\cos\theta)^2 \).
This doubles the circle-of-radius-\(a\) area rather than expanding the square.
This uses a bare sector formula instead of integrating the squared cardioid.
This triples \( \pi a^2 \) instead of using the correct coefficient \( \tfrac32 \).
This is off by one from the correct total area.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct total area.

stem: The limaçon \( r=[[a]]+[[b]]\cos\theta \) has no inner loop since \( [[a]]>[[b]] \). Find the total area it encloses as \( \theta \) ranges over \( [0,2\pi] \).
distractor_rationales: This ignores the \( b\cos\theta \) term entirely, using only the circle of radius \( a \).
This drops the cross-term contribution, treating the area as if \( a \) and \( b \) were independent circles.
This mistakenly uses \( (a+b)^2 \) as if \( r \) were constant at its maximum value.
This doubles the correct coefficient of \( \pi \).
This is off by one from the correct total area.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct total area.

stem: One petal of the rose \( r=[[a]]\sin([[k]]\theta) \) is traced out for \( \theta\in\left[0,\dfrac{\pi}{[[k]]}\right] \). Find the area enclosed by that single petal.
distractor_rationales: This uses the full-rose sweep \( [0,\pi] \) instead of one petal's range.
This drops the \( \tfrac12 \) factor from the area formula.
This uses \( k^{2}\) in the denominator instead of \( k \).
This multiplies by \( k \) instead of dividing by it.
This is off by one from the correct petal area.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct petal area.

stem: Find the area of the region that lies inside the cardioid \( r=[[a]](1+\cos\theta) \) and outside the circle \( r=[[a]] \), for \( \theta\in\left[-\dfrac{\pi}{2},\dfrac{\pi}{2}\right] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses only the circle's own area instead of the region between the two curves.
This uses the full cardioid-circle area rather than the region between them.
This halves the correct coefficient incorrectly.
This forgets to divide by the leading factor of \( \tfrac14 \).
This is off by one from the correct area.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct area.
